name: Melomakarona Phoenikia
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the honey, granulated sugar, water, and lemon juice in a saucepan. Bring to a boil and stir for 5 minutes.
2. Remove from heat and set aside to cool slightly.
3. In a large bowl, mix together the walnuts, 1/4 cup softened butter, 2 teaspoons cinnamon, and 5 tablespoons of the syrup.
4. Reserve the remaining syrup and set the filling aside while you prepare the dough.
5. Cream the remaining butter with the oil until light and fluffy using an electric mixer.
6. Gradually add the icing sugar, beating on medium speed.
7. Slowly incorporate the orange juice into the batter, followed by the cognac or brandy, 1 teaspoon cinnamon, and the other spices.
8. In a small bowl, sift together the baking powder, baking soda, and 2 cups flour.
9. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the batter and beat until just combined with enough flour to form a soft dough.
10. Knead the dough briefly with your hands.
11. Shape small oblongs of dough about 2-1/2 inches long and 1 inch high, then flatten between palms.
12. Place 1 teaspoon of filling in the center and work the dough around it to enclose completely, pressing firmly to seal.
13. Place shaped cakes on a baking sheet and repeat until all are formed.
14. Bake in a moderate oven (350°F) for 25 minutes, then cool on a rack.
15. Reheat the syrup and dip each cake into it, arranging on a platter. Sprinkle with additional chopped walnuts and cinnamon as desired.
16. Cool before serving. The flavor improves after a few days if stored in advance.
